day1_insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: high tide level is lowering risk — a high tide can mobilize bacteria held in wet sand and push the waterline toward outfalls; hours since high tide is raising risk — the timing relative to high tide affects how concentrated bacteria are at the shoreline; and the lack of recent rain is keeping runoff-driven contamination low. The most recent sample, 5 days ago, was excellent at 5 MPN/100mL. It's a naturally clean, well-flushed open-coast beach (unsafe only ~3% of the time), so outside of storms it's reliably safe.
day2_insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: high tide level is raising risk — a high tide can mobilize bacteria held in wet sand and push the waterline toward outfalls; strong sunlight is lowering risk by inactivating bacteria with UV; and tidal range is raising risk — large tidal swings resuspend bacteria stored in sediment and wet sand. The most recent sample, 3 days ago, was excellent at 5 MPN/100mL. It's a naturally clean, well-flushed open-coast beach (unsafe only ~3% of the time), so outside of storms it's reliably safe.
day3_insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: high tide level is raising risk — a high tide can mobilize bacteria held in wet sand and push the waterline toward outfalls; tidal range is raising risk — large tidal swings resuspend bacteria stored in sediment and wet sand; and onshore wave energy is raising risk — onshore wave energy drives water — and any plume — toward the beach. The last lab sample (5 MPN/100mL) is now 8 days old — beaches are sampled about weekly, so a reading this stale carries little weight, and this estimate leans on current conditions. It's a naturally clean, well-flushed open-coast beach (unsafe only ~3% of the time), so outside of storms it's reliably safe.
